doc_description: USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program overview describing no-money-down, 100% financed rural home loans, borrower and property eligibility, loan terms, and how to apply through approved lenders.

structure.title: Together , America Prospers
structure.summary: The document explains USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers in rural areas. It outlines the program’s purpose, benefits, and main loan features, including the fact that it is not limited to first-time homebuyers and can be used to buy, build, repair, improve, or relocate a home. It describes borrower eligibility requirements such as occupying the home as a primary residence, meeting area income limits, lacking access to conventional financing without PMI, and meeting citizenship and federal program eligibility rules. It also covers property requirements, noting that homes must be in eligible rural areas and that loan amounts are based on repayment ability and appraised value rather than purchase price, with no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit in common circumstances. Additional terms include no required down payment, flexibility with gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates, and the ability to finance certain closing and related costs. The document ends with guidance on how to begin by working with approved USDA lenders and provides references to the governing regulations and handbook.

nodes.title: Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program
nodes.summary:
The document explains USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders offer no-money-down, 100% financed loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers. It describes what the program can be used for, including buying an existing home, repairing or improving a home, relocating a home, or building a new one. It also outlines borrower eligibility requirements, such as living in the home as a primary residence, meeting income limits, being unable to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and meeting citizenship and federal program rules.

The document summarizes property eligibility, noting that homes must be in eligible rural areas and that loan amounts are based on repayment ability and appraised value rather than purchase price, with no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit in typical cases. It highlights additional loan terms, including no required down payment, flexible gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates set with the lender, and the ability to finance certain closing and related costs. It also explains that borrowers must work directly with USDA-approved lenders and provides references to program regulations, the handbook, and online resources for income, property, lenders, and local assistance.

nodes.title: What does this program do?
nodes.summary: The document describes USDA Rural Development’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers. It explains that the program is backed by a 90% loan guarantee to reduce lender risk and can be used to buy, build, or repair an existing home. The document outlines who can apply, including income limits, primary residence requirements, inability to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and citizenship/eligibility rules. It also covers eligible property types, emphasizing rural single-family homes and noting there is no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit if the land is typical for the area. Additional loan terms are described, such as no down payment, allowance for gift funds, fixed 30-year rates, and financing based on appraised value, which may include closing costs and related expenses. Finally, it explains that borrowers must work with approved USDA lenders and references the governing regulations, handbook, and program resources.

nodes.title: Who can apply?
nodes.summary: The document explains USDA’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders offer no-money-down, 100% financed loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers. It describes how the USDA guarantee reduces lender risk, making it easier for people to buy, build, or improve a home, and notes that the program is not limited to first-time homebuyers. The text outlines borrower eligibility requirements, such as occupying the home as a primary residence, meeting local income limits, being unable to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and meeting citizenship/noncitizen and federal program participation rules. It also covers property eligibility, emphasizing that homes must be in approved rural areas and that loan terms are based on repayment ability rather than set price or acreage limits. Additional details include stable income requirements, no down payment, flexible gift funds, fixed 30-year rates, and the ability to finance certain closing and repair costs. Finally, it provides guidance on how to apply through USDA-approved lenders and cites the governing regulations and handbook for the program.

nodes.title: What types of properties are eligible?
nodes.summary: This document explains USDA Rural Development’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders offer 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers. It outlines the program’s purpose, including financing for buying, building, repairing, improving, or relocating a home, and notes that it is not limited to first-time homebuyers. The document describes who can apply, emphasizing primary residence occupancy, income limits, inability to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and citizenship or eligibility requirements. It also covers property eligibility, focusing on homes in approved rural areas, and explains loan terms such as no down payment, flexible use of funds, repayment-based approval, and 30-year fixed interest rates negotiated with lenders. Finally, it provides guidance on how to get started through USDA-approved lenders and lists the regulations and handbook that govern the program.

nodes.title: What are some other loan terms?
nodes.summary:
The document explains the USDA Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down home loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers in rural areas. It describes what the program can be used for, including buying an existing home, building a new home, or financing repairs and rehabilitation.

It outlines basic eligibility requirements, such as occupying the home as a primary residence, meeting local income limits, being unable to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and meeting citizenship or legal status rules. It also explains property requirements, including that homes must be in eligible rural locations and that loans are based on repayment ability and appraised value rather than purchase price.

The document also summarizes key loan terms, such as no required down payment, allowance for gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates, and the ability to finance certain closing and related costs. Finally, it gives guidance on how to apply through USDA-approved lenders and lists the rules and handbook governing the program.

nodes.title: How do we get started?
nodes.summary:
The document explains the USDA Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders offer low- and moderate-income borrowers 100% financed, no-money-down loans backed by a 90% USDA guarantee to reduce lender risk. It outlines what the program can be used for, including buying an existing home, building a new home, or financing repairs, improvements, or relocation costs. It also lists borrower eligibility requirements such as occupying the home as a primary residence, meeting local income limits, being unable to obtain conventional financing without PMI, and meeting citizenship and federal participation rules.

The document describes eligible property requirements, emphasizing that homes must be in approved rural areas, with no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit if the land is typical for the area. It notes that “flipped” properties may be eligible if other rules are met. Additional loan terms are highlighted, including stable income, no down payment, no limit on gift funds, fixed 30-year rates, and the ability to finance certain closing and related costs based on appraised value. It also explains that applicants must work with approved USDA lenders and provides references to program regulations, the handbook, and online resources.

nodes.title: What governs this program?
nodes.summary: The document explains USDA Rural Development’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide no-money-down, 100% financed loans to low- and moderate-income borrowers in eligible rural areas. It outlines who can apply, including income, occupancy, credit/financing, and citizenship requirements, and notes that the program is not limited to first-time homebuyers. It describes eligible property types and conditions, such as single-family homes in rural locations, flexible use for purchase, repair, relocation, or new construction, and the allowance of certain “flipped” properties. The document also summarizes key loan terms, including no down payment, no set maximum purchase price, appraised-value-based financing, possible inclusion of closing costs and repair expenses, and fixed 30-year rates. Finally, it provides guidance on how to begin through USDA-approved lenders and references the governing regulations, handbook, and official USDA resources.

pages.content:
Together , America Prospers 
Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program 
What does this 
program do? 
The Single Family Housing 
Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G) helps approved lenders offer 100 percent-fnanced, no-money-down loans to eligible low-and moderate-income people. 
Backed by USDA Rural 
Development, the 90-percent loan guarantee encourages lender participation by minimizing risk. 
With a Single Family Housing 
loan guarantee, you can buy an existing home, cover the cost of rehabilitating, improving, or relocating a home, or build new. 
This program is not restricted to 
frst-time homebuyers. 
QR code to the SFH-G webpage Who can apply? 
Among other requirements, you must: • Agree to occupy the home as your 
primary residence 
• Meet median household income requirements for the area in which the eligible home is located. You will fnd income eligibility information at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-income. 
• Be unable to get conventional fnancing with no private mortgage insurance (PMI) 
• Meet U.S. citizen or noncitizen requirements, and not be suspended or debarred from participating in federal programs 
What types of properties are eligible? 
Among other requirements, single-
family dwellings must be located in eligible rural areas. A property eligibility map is available at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-property. 
• Loans are based on repayment 
ability , and there is no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit as long as acreage is considered common for the area. 
• Provided they are otherwise eligible, “fipped” properties are allowed. What are some other loan terms? 
• You must have stable, dependable 
income and demonstrate a willingness and ability to repay debt. 
• No down payment is required, and there is no limit on gift funds. 
• Fixed, 30-year-interest rates are negotiated between you and your approved lender . 
• Loans are based on a property’s appraised value, not purchase price. Thus, you potentially can also include closing costs, home repair expenses, and such items as utilities connection fees, tax and insurance escrow, essential household equipment, and site preparation in your fnancing. 
How do we get started? 
• You must work directly with 
approved USDA RD lenders. A state-based list is available at this link: rd.usda.gov/resources/lenders 
What governs this program? 
• 7-CFR Part 3555, available at this 
link: tinyurl.com/7CFR-3555 
• The SFH-G Handbook is available at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-HB - PDF 
NOTE: Because information changes, always consult offcial program instructions 
or contact your local Rural Development offce for help. A list is available at this link: go.usa.gov/xJHPE. You will fnd additional resources, forms, and program information at rd.usda.gov. USDA is an equal opportunity provider, employer, and lender. 
Last revised October 2024
